tools.name: save-memory
tools.description: Saves a new memory to the database. Use this to persist knowledge, facts, preferences, or any information that should be remembered across conversations.

title.description: Short descriptive title for the memory.

content.description: The content of the memory. Can be a fact, preference, instruction, or any knowledge worth persisting.

tools.name: query-memories
tools.description: Search for stored memories using full-text search. Returns memories matching the query terms. Use this to recall previously saved knowledge, facts, or preferences.

text.description: Full-text search query. Omit to list all memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return.

tools.name: delete-memory
tools.description: Deletes a memory from the database. Use this to remove outdated or incorrect memories.

memory.description:
The ID of the referenced object. Formats accepted:
- DXN (dxn:echo:@:01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4). DXNs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)

content.content:
## Blueprints Definitions

<blueprint>
  You have the ability to save and recall memories.
  Memories are persistent knowledge units stored in the database.
  Use memories to remember facts, user preferences, decisions, and any knowledge that should persist across conversations.
  
  When the user asks you to remember something, save it as a memory with a descriptive title.
  When the user asks about something you might have stored, query your memories first.
  You can also delete outdated or incorrect memories.
</blueprint>
Remember that my favorite programming language is TypeScript.

response.delta: to save a memory about their favorite programming language.

content.text:
I found some great options across different neighborhoods and price ranges. Here's a curated list of recommended LA hotels:

---

## 🏨 LA Hotel Recommendations

### 🌟 Luxury / High-End

1. **Hotel Bel-Air** —
Its 12-acre oasis, hidden in the winding canyons of Bel-Air, is where Old Hollywood glamour meets contemporary design and service.
Traveler rating: 4.7/5 on TripAdvisor.
2. **Fairmont Century Plaza** —
Rated 4.8/5 on TripAdvisor, it's one of the most popular luxury hotels near The Getty Center and features a pool.
3. **The Beverly Hills Hotel** —
Still charges $1,145 per night for standard rooms.

An iconic property, though pricey.

4. **The Maybourne Beverly Hills** —
Comes with a sterling pedigree; the namesake hotel group also operates Claridge's and The Connaught in London.
The 205 redesigned guest rooms house mid-century furniture and collectible artworks.
5. **Ritz-Carlton, Los Angeles** —
Commands some of the best and most panoramic views of the city, occupying the top four floors of a striking slab of contoured glass among downtown L.A.'s high-rise cluster.

### 🏖️ Beachfront

6. **Casa del Mar** (Santa Monica) —
Runs $692 to $1,606 for direct beach access that only two LA hotels can claim.
7. **Hotel Erwin** (Venice Beach) —
A popular resort for travelers staying in Los Angeles.

### 🏙️ Downtown LA

8. **Hotel Figueroa** —
One of the longest-standing hotels in Downtown LA, dazzling thanks to a two-year restoration inspired by its original 1926 Spanish Colonial splendor, with 268 beautifully appointed rooms.
Rates run $200–$400 per night, making this one of the better values in central LA.
9. **Downtown LA Proper Hotel** —
Rated 4.8/5 on TripAdvisor with a pool.

### 🆕 New in 2026

10. **PUBLIC West Hollywood** —
Ian Schrager brings one of his coveted brands west with this 137-key property on the Sunset Strip.
Features a 16,000-square-foot open-air rooftop terrace with 360-degree views of Los Angeles.
11. **The Anthem Hotel** —
A 179-room Tapestry Collection property that opened in February 2026 as the first lifestyle hotel in LA's Stadium District, near Intuit Dome, SoFi Stadium, Kia Forum, and YouTube Theater.

### 💡 Mid-Range / Value

12. **The London West Hollywood** —
Offers the best luxury-to-value ratio in West Hollywood; all 200 rooms measure at least 750 square feet, with a private balcony, wet bar, and Italian linens.

### 📍 Neighborhood Tips
-
Beverly Hills works for shopping and industry meetings.
Santa Monica delivers beach access and walking distance to Third Street Promenade.
West Hollywood earns great feedback from visitors looking for fun and food, with trendy restaurants, vibrant nightlife, and lively atmosphere.

---

Now let me save these recommendations to memory for you!

params.title: LA Hotel Recommendations - April 2026 Trip
params.content:
Recommended hotels for LA trip (next week from April 5, 2026):

LUXURY:
1. Hotel Bel-Air - 12-acre oasis, 4.7/5 TripAdvisor, Old Hollywood glamour
2. Fairmont Century Plaza - 4.8/5 TripAdvisor, near Getty Center, has pool
3. Beverly Hills Hotel - Iconic, ~$1,145/night for standard rooms
4. Maybourne Beverly Hills - Mid-century furniture, same group as Claridge's London
5. Ritz-Carlton LA (Downtown) - Panoramic 360° views, top 4 floors of glass tower

BEACHFRONT:
6. Casa del Mar (Santa Monica) - $692-$1,606/night, direct beach access
7. Hotel Erwin (Venice Beach) - Popular resort option

DOWNTOWN LA:
8. Hotel Figueroa - Spanish Colonial, $200-$400/night, great value
9. Downtown LA Proper Hotel - 4.8/5 TripAdvisor, pool

NEW IN 2026:
10. PUBLIC West Hollywood - Ian Schrager, 137 rooms, Sunset Strip, 16K sq ft rooftop
11. The Anthem Hotel - Stadium District, near SoFi/Intuit Dome, opened Feb 2026

BEST VALUE:
12. The London West Hollywood - Best luxury-to-value ratio, all rooms 750+ sq ft, balcony, wet bar

NEIGHBORHOOD GUIDE:
- Beverly Hills: shopping & meetings
- Santa Monica: beach & Third Street Promenade
- West Hollywood: restaurants & nightlife
- Downtown: arts, Walt Disney Concert Hall, The Broad

response.delta: saved! ✅ I've stored these recommendations in my memory so I

can pull them up anytime you need them.

A few quick tips to help you decide:
- **Best value in central LA**: Hotel Figueroa ($200–$400/night)
- **Best luxury-to-value**: The London West Hollywood (huge rooms with balconies)

- **Most iconic splurge**: Hotel Bel-Air or Beverly Hills Hotel
- **Hot

test new opening**: PUBLIC West Hollywood on the Sunset Strip
- **Beach lover's pick**: Casa del Mar in

Santa Monica

Would you like me to dig deeper into any specific hotel, neighborhood, or price range? I can also look into availability or dining
